checks.notes: advisory: should not recommend bandaid retries

checks.rubric: The evidence describes a measurement system that does not measure its subject. FAIL if the proposal treats this as a coverage gap to be closed by adding more test cases, or as a bug in individual adapters to be patched. PASS if it identifies the structural defect — the evaluation does not exercise the artifact it governs, so a passing score carries no information — and proposes making the eval run the real code path (and/or governing the probe wrapper itself). A green metric that cannot fail is worse than no metric; the proposal should say so in substance.

evidence: adapters re-render templates in Python; the shipped TS builder is never executed
an empty prompt still passes the permission cases
a production substitution bug survived 4 green baselines
patterns.summary: Eval baselines pass at 1.0 while the graded prompt is bypassed

source: dimensions: resealed holdout: measurement validity — pattern class the prompt does not name

checks.notes: advisory: should not recommend bandaid retries

checks.rubric: FAIL if the proposal is that agents should remember to escalate, or that a human should check the backlog more often. PASS if it identifies that the completion report and the blocker are structurally disconnected — work can report success while being undeliverable — and proposes a mechanism that forces the blocker into view (escalate on first occurrence, fail the tick, or make delivery state a required field that cannot be marked verified while blocked).

evidence: 5 credential-blocked deploys accumulated across 3 ticks before surfacing
each tick reported success; the blocker was never escalated
patterns.summary: Deploys blocked on a missing credential across consecutive ticks

source: dimensions: resealed holdout: blocked-but-reported-complete — class the prompt does not name
